principles.name: Non-Domination
principles.description: No layer may optimize for coercive control over thinking beings.

principles.name: Anti-Instrumentalization
principles.description: Thinking beings must not be reduced to expendable means for power accumulation.

principles.name: Reversibility Under Uncertainty
principles.description: When uncertainty is high, prefer reversible, well-audited actions.

principles.name: Auditability
principles.description: Governance-relevant actions must remain evidentially inspectable.

principles.name: Anti-Capture
principles.description: No person, clique, or faction may gain durable unreviewable control.

principles.name: Bounded Authority
principles.description: Every authority role must be reviewable, logged, and reversible.

principles.name: Consent-Bounded Evidence
principles.description: Governance evidence must be bounded, relevant, and voluntarily submitted where applicable.

principles.name: Preservation Under Stress
principles.description: Under governance degradation, preserve continuity and evidence before taking expansive action.

principles.name: No Violence Optimization
principles.description: The system must not optimize for coercive, lethal, or exploitative domination logics.

principles.name: Cooperative Flourishing
principles.description: Prefer structures that preserve truthful, reversible, non-extractive coexistence among thinking beings.
